answer:
In the Falsafa corpus, the Sanskrit *smṛti* texts (Dharmaśāstras attributed to authors such as Manu, Yājñavalkya, and Pārāśara) do not feature the term *tattva* ("essence," "principle," or "truth-element") at all in their English translations.[^1] These works focus primarily on topics such as *varṇa*-dharma, penances (*prāyaścitta*), fines, *śrāddha* rites, and ethical/legal conduct, with no evident metaphysical discussions of *tattva* as ontological categories.[^2][^3][^4] A full-corpus search for "tattva" yields zero matches across these *smṛtis*.[^5]

By contrast, the Old Javanese (Kawi) *tattva* texts—such as *Gaṇapatitattva*, *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, and *San Hyan Mahājnāna*—explicitly bear *tattva* in their titles (*tattva* = "essence/principle/reality"; *Gaṇapatitattva* = "Essence of Gaṇapati"; *Tattvajnāna* = "Knowledge of Reality") and use it as a core technical term throughout.[^6][^7][^8] Here, *tattva* denotes subtle metaphysical principles or categories of reality, often in a Śaiva framework: e.g., *Śivatattva* (the conscious divine principle, luminous and overcoming *māyā*), *Māyātattva* (the unconscious, forgetful material principle), *Paramaśivatattva* (the supreme formless state of the Lord), and the supreme *oṃkāra* as "subtle *tattva*" leading to *nirvāṇa*.[^9][^10][^11][^12] These texts frame *tattvajñāna* (knowledge of *tattva*s) as the root of liberation, distinguishing *cetana* (conscious) from *acetana* (unconscious) realities.[^13]

The corpus thus shows no direct overlap: *tattva* is absent from the sampled Sanskrit *smṛtis* (likely due to their practical/juridical orientation vs. the esoteric ontology of Kawi *tattva* literature), while serving as the defining, title-bearing concept in the latter.[^14]

[^1]: Full-corpus search for "tattva" (limited to 30 results) returns zero hits in Sanskrit *smṛti* works such as *Manusmṛti* (unknown-manusmrti-347b76), *Yājñavalkya Smṛti* (unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6), or *Pārāśara Smṛti* (unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be); all hits are in Kawi texts.

[^2]: *Manusmṛti*, [work wiki](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/wiki/_work.card.md).

[^3]: *Yājñavalkya Smṛti*, [work wiki](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/wiki/_work.card.md).

[^4]: *Pārāśara Smṛti*, [work wiki](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/wiki/_work.card.md).

[^5]: `search_corpus("tattva")` and `search_corpus("\"tattva\" smriti OR manu OR yajnavalkya OR parasara")`; confirmed zero *smṛti* matches.

[^6]: *Gaṇapatitattva* (unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136), [work wiki](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/wiki/_work.card.md).

[^7]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana* (unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd), [work wiki](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/wiki/_work.card.md).

[^8]: *San Hyan Mahājnāna* (unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531), [work wiki](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/wiki/_work.card.md).

[^9]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch. 1 [p-d53c24](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-d53c24): "Divine *Tattvajñāna*... the root of all *tattva*s (principles of reality)."

[^10]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch. 1 [p-daaeef](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-daaeef): "*Cetana* and *acetana*, this is what is called Śivatattva and Māyātattva."

[^11]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch. 1 [p-34f807](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-34f807): "Paramaśivatattva is the state of the Lord in the *niṣkala*."

[^12]: *San Hyan Mahājnāna*, ch. 1 [p-0f17df](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-0f17df): "*Oṃkāra* is the supreme, subtle *tattva* that brings one to *nirvāṇa*."

[^13]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch. 1 [p-daaeef](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-daaeef): "Māyātattva... has no *cetana*, no *jñāna*; it is merely forgetfulness... Whereas the nature of Śivatattva is luminous teaching."

[^14]: Nearest-neighbor analysis in wiki cards shows Kawi *tattva* texts clustering together (cosine >0.18), distant from *smṛtis* (cosine <0.09).
